primary_purpose: Defines low-level constants, data layout and resource structures (VRAM, RAM, audio, map, sprites, etc.) used throughout TIC-80. Acts as central memory schema spec.

key_structures.name: tic_tile / tic_sprites / tic_tiles
key_structures.description: Fixed-size byte arrays representing one or many 8×8 sprite graphics tiles.

key_structures.name: tic_waveform / tic_waveforms
key_structures.description: Raw PCM waveform tables and collection of waveforms for audio synthesis.

key_structures.name: tic_sample / tic_samples
key_structures.description: Sound effect definition: per tick parameters plus loop metadata for one SFX, and array container for all SFX.

key_structures.name: tic_track, tic_tracks, tic_patterns
key_structures.description: Music sequencing data: patterns/frames mapping channels to SFX and tempo/speed parameters.

key_structures.name: tic_bank and tic_cartridge
key_structures.description: Hierarchical container for all cartridge resources including multiple memory banks, code blob and binary attachments.

key_structures.name: tic_ram
key_structures.description: Unified runtime RAM view exposing strongly-typed sub-structures (VRAM, audio registers, etc.) overlaying a contiguous byte array.

key_structures.name: tic_vram
key_structures.description: 16 K video memory segment containing framebuffer, palette, color mapping and viewport variables.

memory_regions.name: VRAM (tic_vram)
memory_regions.purpose: Screen buffer, palette, sprite sheet viewport parameters and related registers.

memory_regions.name: RAM (tic_ram)
memory_regions.purpose: Complete runtime memory used by console, including VRAM, sprites, map, audio, state and user space.

memory_regions.name: Persistent memory
memory_regions.purpose: Save data slots exposed via pmem API.

memory_regions.name: Cartridge banks
memory_regions.purpose: ROM-like storage for resources and bytecode across multiple banks.

notes: Accurate mirroring of these bit-packed structs is essential for binary compatibility with existing cartridge files. Many fields use endianness-dependent bitfields; Swift lacks native bitfields, so manual bitmask helpers or custom property wrappers will be needed. Consider wrapping underlying `Data` buffers with accessor structs to avoid ABI mismatch.
